hot_topics.topic: Trump declares Iran ceasefire over amid US strikes
hot_topics.synthesis: President Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran "over" following an exchange of strikes, including U.S. attacks on Iranian military boats, exacerbating tensions in the region. Despite his strong rhetoric, analysts suggest that diplomatic talks remain the most viable path forward for the administration. This escalation strains the existing agreement and highlights ongoing challenges in international relations.

hot_topics.topic: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner faces pressure to withdraw over sexual assault allegation
hot_topics.synthesis: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner is facing immense pressure from top Democrats, including Bernie Sanders and the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, to withdraw from the race following a sexual assault allegation. While Platner denies the "categorically false" accusation, his campaign is also accused of trying to manipulate the process for selecting his replacement. This controversy has led to a significant withdrawal of support from party leadership and endorsements.

hot_topics.topic: Charlie Kirk assassination trial confronts conspiracy theories
hot_topics.synthesis: Tyler Robinson, the accused in Charlie Kirk's assassination, is undergoing a preliminary hearing in Utah, where video evidence and pre-shooting interactions are being presented. Douglas Murray emphasizes that the trial is a test of truth against numerous unsubstantiated conspiracy theories propagated by online commentators, including former associates of Kirk, who have attempted to divert blame from Robinson.

hot_topics.topic: Google Pixel lineup faces potential price increases for August 12 launch
hot_topics.synthesis: Google has scheduled its "Made By Google" event for August 12, where it is expected to unveil new Pixel devices. Reports from multiple sources suggest that the entire Pixel lineup, including phones and smartwatches, could see price increases this year. These potential price hikes are attributed to factors like an "AI-driven component shortage" and a rumored upgrade to 256GB as the base storage option for Pixel phones.

hot_topics.topic: Apple partners with Broadcom for increased US chip manufacturing
hot_topics.synthesis: Apple has signed a multi-year, $30 billion+ deal with Broadcom to boost the production of U.S.-made custom wireless connectivity chips for its products. This significant investment aims to produce over 15 billion chips domestically, aligning with initiatives to strengthen local manufacturing and reduce reliance on overseas supply chains.

hot_topics.topic: Netflix expands content with shorter videos from digital publishers
hot_topics.synthesis: Netflix is expanding its streaming library by integrating shorter video content, ranging from 2 to 20 minutes, through new partnerships with digital media brands like BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, Rolling Stone, and Variety. This initiative aims to allow subscribers to access a broader array of licensed past videos and new ongoing series directly on the platform without having to navigate to other online sources.

hot_topics.topic: Marine Le Pen confirms French presidential bid despite embezzlement conviction
hot_topics.synthesis: French nationalist leader Marine Le Pen has confirmed her candidacy for the 2027 presidential election, launching her campaign shortly after a court upheld her embezzlement conviction. Although her sentence was reduced and did not bar her from seeking office, she also announced plans to appeal the conviction.

items.title: Sangre violenta / sangre violeta
items.summary: A video explores how diverse individuals are uniting in resistance and protest against the harrowing reality of gendered violence. It highlights collective action in the face of widespread femicide in Mexico.

items.title: Bethesda, id Software reportedly hit hard by Microsoft layoffs
items.summary: Microsoft's recent layoffs, announced for the Xbox division, reportedly significantly impacted game developers id Software and Bethesda. Sources indicate that up to 50% of id Software's team, including many coders, were let go, affecting about 90 employees.

items.title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
items.summary: US manufacturers, especially in the Rust Belt, face significantly higher electricity costs due to increased demand from AI data centers, impacting profit margins. This energy strain could undermine President Trump's "Made in America" manufacturing revival plan, despite his support for tech companies.

items.title: Google's Pixel 11 launch event is set for August 12, with possible price increases
items.summary: Google announced its "Made By Google" event for August 12, where it's expected to launch new Pixel phones with similar designs but possibly higher prices due to an "AI-driven component shortage." Rumors also suggest the base storage for smaller phones will increase to 256GB, and new "Pixel Glow" notification lights.

items.title: Ocean rift zone saw spreading happen in a sudden burst
items.summary: New data from a monitoring system on the Australian and Antarctic plates shows that most seafloor spreading occurs in relatively short bursts. Key events were observed without obvious seismic activity, providing new insights into plate tectonics.

items.title: SCOTUS lets Texas enforce app store law that Big Tech calls "censorship regime"
items.summary: The Supreme Court declined to block a Texas law requiring age verification for app stores, allowing its enforcement while legal challenges continue. A lower federal judge had previously issued an injunction, deeming the Texas App Store Accountability Act a likely First Amendment violation.

items.title: Surprisingly large number of people may have marker for tick-linked meat allergy
items.summary: A study indicates that up to 30% of people in some US regions may carry the antibody for alpha-gal syndrome, a tick-borne red meat allergy, far exceeding known allergy cases. This suggests a larger population is at risk than previously estimated and highlights diagnostic challenges.

items.title: The Nintendo Switch's days are numbered—but what is that number?
items.summary: Nintendo will stop selling the original Switch in Europe next February due to new EU regulations requiring easily replaceable batteries. While not affecting other regions, this move prompts speculation about the 9-year-old console's longevity given the impending Switch 2 release.

items.title: The Weather Channel increases streaming subscription prices by up to $20
items.summary: The Weather Channel's dedicated smart TV app has increased its streaming subscription prices by 66.7%, from $3 to $5 per month or $30 to $50 annually. This app offers livestreaming, on-demand shows, local forecasts, and other features without a cable subscription.

items.title: Two teens learn the hard way not to do toy gun drivebys from a Waymo
items.summary: Two California teenagers were detained by police after using a Waymo robotaxi for toy gun drive-bys and drinking, violating rider rules. Waymo stopped the car and alerted authorities, marking another instance of the autonomous vehicle reporting misbehaving passengers.

items.title: 'The water just came so fast': Typhoon triggers floods and rare tornadoes in China
items.summary: A typhoon in China triggered widespread floods and rare tornadoes, leaving villagers stranded and struggling to obtain food and aid. The rapid onset of the disaster caused significant devastation and hardship.

items.title: Australia dock workers call for 28-hour week in AI talks
items.summary: Australian dock workers are advocating for a 28-hour work week during negotiations over the implementation of AI and automation in ports. The union highlights concerns that workers are "in the crosshairs" of these technological advancements.

items.title: Bowen: For all his bluster, Trump has no better option than talks with Iran
items.summary: BBC International Editor Jeremy Bowen suggests that despite President Trump's strong rhetoric, negotiations remain the most viable option for dealing with Iran, even as recent US strikes demonstrate the fragility of any agreement. The path to a resolution is fraught with difficulty.

items.title: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
items.summary: Cubans protested after experiencing the third nationwide power cut this year, with many remaining in the dark despite official claims of restoration. The frequent outages have led to widespread anger among the population.

items.title: Eight killed after landslide hits girls' school in Bangladesh
items.summary: A landslide caused by heavy monsoon rains in southeastern Bangladesh killed eight people after hitting a girls' school. Rescuers were working to recover bodies from the mud.

items.title: Fears of a massacre in this city on the front line of Sudan's war
items.summary: The UN human rights chief expressed fears of a potential massacre in El-Obeid, a city in Sudan that has been under "siege-like conditions" for 18 months. The situation highlights the severe humanitarian crisis in the region.

items.title: German doctor jailed for murder of 15 patients and suspected of more
items.summary: A German palliative care doctor was sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of 15 patients, with suspicions of further killings. The conviction highlights a grave case of medical misconduct.

items.title: Khamenei's coffin carried through Shia shrines as ceremonies held in Iraq
items.summary: The coffin of Iran's late supreme leader, Khamenei, was transported through Shia shrines in Najaf and Karbala, Iraq, as funeral ceremonies continued for a fifth day. This marks a significant religious and political event.

items.title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail
items.summary: A lawyer for Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya, a detained Gaza doctor, claims his client was severely beaten in an Israeli jail, to the extent that he was unrecognizable. This report raises concerns about the treatment of prisoners.

items.title: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
items.summary: NATO allies announced a £37 billion commitment for a new missile project, with Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer convening leaders to discuss the program. This significant investment bolsters the alliance's defense capabilities.

items.title: New video shows man believed to be Tyler Robinson before Charlie Kirk shooting
items.summary: New video footage presented during a preliminary hearing appears to show Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk, walking through a parking garage and running across a roof before the shooting. This evidence is part of the ongoing court proceedings.

items.title: Nigeria's president demands to know how fake agency was allegedly set up in his office
items.summary: Nigeria's president has demanded an explanation for how a fake agency was allegedly set up in his office, with an individual forging appointment letters and securing funding. This incident highlights issues of fraud and corruption at high levels.

items.title: Russian fuel shortages bite – but will Putin change tack in Ukraine war?
items.summary: Russia is experiencing fuel shortages, even in Moscow, raising questions about whether economic pressure will influence Putin's strategy in the Ukraine war. It remains uncertain if these shortages will lead to negotiations or an escalation of conflict.

items.title: Suspect ate Chick-fil-A and interacted with Charlie Kirk staff before shooting, court hears
items.summary: During a preliminary hearing, details emerged that Tyler Robinson, accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, ate at Chick-fil-A and interacted with Kirk's staff before the shooting. The hearing will determine if Robinson stands trial.

items.title: Teenager arrested after two girls, 13, seriously injured in German school attack
items.summary: A teenager was arrested following a serious school attack in Schongau, Bavaria, where two 13-year-old girls were seriously injured. A major police operation was conducted in the area.

items.title: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
items.summary: Leading Democrats are urging Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner to withdraw from the race following a sexual assault allegation, which Platner denies as "categorically false." Platner stated he is reflecting on his path forward.

items.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
items.summary: Australia's largest telecoms company experienced a major outage, affecting train services and emergency calls, with servers in Sydney and Melbourne data centers identified as the cause. The exact reason for the outage remains unknown.

items.title: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes
items.summary: President Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran "over" following an exchange of strikes between the US and Iran, but kept open the possibility of future talks. This statement comes amidst escalating tensions and challenges to the agreement to end the war.

items.title: US strikes target Iranian military boats
items.summary: The US launched strikes against Iranian military boats in response to attacks on three oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. These actions mark an escalation in tensions between the two nations.

items.title: Utah boarding school where Paris Hilton alleged abuse as a teen loses its licence
items.summary: A Utah boarding school, where Paris Hilton previously alleged abuse as a teenager, has lost its license due to regulatory citations for neglect and unnecessary restraints. Hilton commented that the facility "failed the children."

items.title: Watch: Gaza football fans watch World Cup from rubble
items.summary: Hundreds of football fans in Gaza were shown watching a World Cup match between Egypt and Argentina amidst rubble. The scene underscores the ongoing challenges faced by residents in the war-torn region.

items.title: Watch: Marine Le Pen confirms run for French presidency
items.summary: Marine Le Pen has confirmed her bid for the French presidency and stated her intention to appeal an embezzlement conviction. This announcement marks her continued presence in the French political landscape.

items.title: Will Le Pen rise again? French nationalist leader defiant after court's ruling
items.summary: Marine Le Pen, the French nationalist leader, launched her presidential campaign just hours after a court confirmed her embezzlement conviction, though her sentence was reduced. She remains defiant despite the legal challenges.

items.title: Apple to increase spend with Broadcom to produce billions more U.S. chips
items.summary: Apple announced an increased investment with Broadcom to produce billions more U.S.-made chips, strengthening domestic manufacturing. This move aligns with broader efforts to onshore technology production.

items.title: Chat Control 1.0 and 2.0 Explained
items.summary: An explanation of "Chat Control 1.0 and 2.0" details proposed regulations for monitoring online communications. These measures aim to address illicit content but raise concerns about privacy and surveillance.

items.title: Decoding the obfuscated bash script on a Uniqlo t-shirt
items.summary: An article details the process of decoding an obfuscated bash script found on a Uniqlo t-shirt, supplied by CDN Akamai to consumers. This unusual discovery highlights hidden code in unexpected places.

items.title: GAO: DOE Is Prematurely Excluding Less Expensive Options for Nuclear Cleanup
items.summary: The Government Accountability Office (GAO) criticized the Department of Energy (DOE) for prematurely dismissing less expensive options for nuclear cleanup. This suggests potential inefficiencies and higher costs in government waste management projects.

items.title: How to Build a Minimal ZFS NAS Without Synology, QNAP, TrueNAS (2024)
items.summary: An article provides instructions on building a minimal ZFS NAS without relying on commercial solutions like Synology, QNAP, or TrueNAS. It offers a DIY approach to network-attached storage using ZFS.

items.title: LineageOS Statistics
items.summary: A webpage provides statistics for LineageOS, an open-source Android distribution. The data offers insights into its user base and device adoption.

items.title: Local, CPU-Friendly, High-Quality TTS (Text-to-Speech) with Kokoro
items.summary: An article introduces Kokoro, a solution for local, CPU-friendly, high-quality Text-to-Speech (TTS) conversion. This tool aims to provide efficient and accessible speech synthesis without heavy hardware requirements.

items.title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs Video Lectures (1986)
items.summary: A collection of video lectures from 1986 on "Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs" (SICP) is available from MIT OpenCourseWare. These classic lectures offer foundational computer science education.

items.title: Tenda firmware (multiple versions) contains hidden authentication backdoor
items.summary: Multiple versions of Tenda firmware have been found to contain a hidden authentication backdoor, posing a significant security risk. This vulnerability could allow unauthorized access to affected devices.

items.title: Former Maine Sen. Troy Jackson says it would be 'self-serving' if Graham Platner runs
items.summary: Former Maine state Senator Troy Jackson filed to replace Graham Platner on the U.S. Senate ballot, stating it would be "self-serving" if Platner continued his run after a sexual assault accusation. Jackson's move follows calls for Platner to drop out.

items.title: Greetings from a Syrian church, infused with a mix of sweet fruit and diverse faiths
items.summary: Syria's Mar Musa monastery is known for its mulberries and unique services that blend Eastern Orthodox Christian and Sufi customs. It represents a place where diverse faiths and traditions converge.

items.title: Here are the 2026 Emmy nominations
items.summary: The 2026 Emmy nominations were announced, with winners slated to be revealed at the 78th Emmy Awards on September 14. Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller presented this year's nominees.

items.title: Maine Democrats say Platner's campaign is trying to influence replacement process
items.summary: Maine Democrats accused Graham Platner's campaign of attempting to influence the process of replacing him on the Senate ballot, an accusation his team denies. This comes amid calls for Platner to withdraw following a sexual assault allegation.

items.title: This factory was severely short on workers. Then it offered flexible work
items.summary: A GE Appliances plant in rural Georgia addressed a severe worker shortage by implementing a flexible work option, allowing employees to sign up for shifts via an app. This approach has significantly eased staffing challenges that emerged during COVID-19.

items.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'over' as NATO summit wraps
items.summary: President Trump announced the ceasefire with Iran is "over" following recent US-Iran attacks, an escalation that strains the agreement to end the war. His statement came as the NATO summit concluded.

items.title: Trump says he believes the Iran ceasefire is 'over.' And, IOC lifts Russia's suspension
items.summary: President Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire "over" but didn't rule out future talks, following an exchange of attacks. Separately, the International Olympic Committee lifted Russia's suspension, allowing its athletes to compete in the 2028 games.

items.title: What's behind the push to make peptide therapies more readily available
items.summary: There's a growing push to make unproven peptide therapies, marketed for longevity and wellness, more accessible, despite being sold through a grey online market. Compounding pharmacies argue they should be allowed to legally produce these to meet demand.

items.title: Bernie Sanders says he told Graham Platner to ‘step aside’
items.summary: Sen. Bernie Sanders stated he advised Maine Democrat Graham Platner to end his Senate bid after a woman accused Platner of sexual assault. This move follows a widespread call from Democrats for Platner to withdraw, despite Sanders previously supporting him through other scandals.

items.title: Most Senate Dems say Platner should drop out. Here's where they all stand.
items.summary: More than half of the Senate Democratic caucus, including top leaders and former allies, have called for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner to drop out of the race following a sexual assault allegation. The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee has also pulled its financial support.

items.title: North Dakota leaders talk Trump & Teddy Roosevelt over bison burgers
items.summary: Leaders in North Dakota discussed President Trump and Teddy Roosevelt while eating bison burgers. The conversation covered political figures and local culture.

items.title: github-code Web Component
items.summary: An experimental `github-code` Web Component has been developed using GPT-5.5 to embed specific code ranges from GitHub URLs. This tool fetches and displays code segments with line numbers, although without syntax highlighting.

items.title: sqlite-migrate 0.2
items.summary: sqlite-migrate 0.2 is a compatibility shim that retires the standalone library, with its functionalities now integrated into sqlite-utils 4.0. Existing projects depending on sqlite-migrate should continue to function with the updated dependency.

items.title: sqlite-utils 4.0
items.summary: sqlite-utils 4.0 is a new release that includes significant updates, detailed in an accompanying blog post, which focuses on database schema migrations. This version marks a major enhancement for the SQLite utility library.

items.title: sqlite-utils 4.0, now with database schema migrations
items.summary: sqlite-utils 4.0 introduces major features including database schema migrations, nested transactions, and support for compound foreign keys. This release significantly enhances the utility for managing SQLite database schemas programmatically.

items.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
items.summary: AI chip maker SambaNova has secured $1 billion in funding, achieving an $11 billion valuation, only five months after its previous major investment round. This rapid fundraising follows earlier rumors of a potential acquisition by Intel for approximately $1.6 billion.

items.title: Apple to produce Made in America wireless chips with Broadcom
items.summary: Apple has finalized a multi-year deal exceeding $30 billion with Broadcom to design and produce custom U.S.-made wireless connectivity chips. This partnership aims to produce over 15 billion chips for Apple products domestically.

items.title: Blue Origin reportedly raising $10B at $130B valuation
items.summary: Blue Origin is reportedly raising $10 billion at a $130 billion pre-money valuation, with investments from Coatue Asset Management, Jeff Bezos, and other major investors. This significant funding round signals strong confidence in the space company.

items.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
items.summary: Claude Cowork, an AI coding agent, has expanded its availability to mobile and web platforms, enabling users to manage tasks from various devices. This update allows for continuous workflow across different computing environments.

items.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
items.summary: Discord acknowledged an AI moderation bug that wrongfully banned users for harmless images, an issue affecting accounts since May and leading to 200 additional bans over a weekend. The company has since identified and fixed the problem.

items.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
items.summary: Figma has acquired the team behind a Y Combinator-backed company that initially developed a vibe-coding platform and later an agent-creation product. This acquisition suggests Figma's interest in expanding its collaborative design tools with new coding or AI functionalities.

items.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
items.summary: Google has scheduled its next Pixel event for August 12, where new Pixel devices are expected to be unveiled. This event will showcase the latest advancements in the company's hardware lineup.

items.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
items.summary: French AI startup ZML has released ZML/LLMD, a free software designed to accelerate AI inference across multiple chips, potentially reducing AI operational costs. The company is endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun.

items.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
items.summary: Meta has launched Muse Image, a new AI image generator with applications in advertising and decoration, but users are already expressing concerns over the use of their photos. The tool offers creator-based opportunities despite initial user pushback.

items.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
items.summary: Microsoft is reducing its AI spending by increasingly relying on its own models, aligning with a broader trend among Silicon Valley giants to cut AI costs. This strategy aims to optimize resource allocation and enhance internal development.

items.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
items.summary: Netflix is expanding its content library by adding 2- to 20-minute videos through new partnerships with digital publishers like Rolling Stone and Variety. This move allows subscribers to access a broader range of shorter-form content directly on the platform.

items.title: Venus Williams-backed WeWard can now lock your apps until you hit your steps
items.summary: WeWard, a French app backed by Venus Williams, has introduced a new feature that can lock user apps until daily step goals are achieved. The company claims its platform increases walking time by nearly 25%.

items.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
items.summary: The success of open-source AI models is not currently harming frontier labs like Anthropic, as both seem to address different phases of the AI lifecycle. This suggests a symbiotic rather than competitive relationship between the two types of models.

items.title: Douglas Murray: Truth Is on Trial in the Charlie Kirk Assassination Case
items.summary: Douglas Murray contends that conspiracy theories surrounding Charlie Kirk's assassination are being challenged by evidence at Tyler Robinson's preliminary hearing. Despite surveillance footage and a confession, some online commentators have redirected blame from Robinson to various other parties.

items.title: Douglas Murray: Truth Is on Trial in the Charlie Kirk Case. Plus. . .
items.summary: Douglas Murray asserts that truth is on trial in the Charlie Kirk assassination case, as suspect Tyler Robinson's preliminary hearing confronts baseless conspiracy theories spread by online commentators. The hearing aims to determine if sufficient evidence exists to proceed to trial.

items.title: Graham Platner Is Bad. But Nobody’s Perfect!
items.summary: This article uses a cinematic analogy to discuss the ongoing Graham Platner scandal, suggesting a "sunk cost fallacy" in how his past issues are perceived. It critiques the idea that "nobody's perfect" should excuse serious allegations.

items.title: Rahm Emanuel Bows to the Left on Israel
items.summary: Rahm Emanuel plans to deliver a critical speech in Israel, signaling a shift to align with progressive views on U.S.-Israeli relations, particularly concerning "needless war-making" and Palestinian lives. His remarks aim to position him as a trusted figure among American progressives ahead of a potential presidential bid.

items.title: ‘Jackass’ Was the Last Great Boys’ Show
items.summary: The article praises "Jackass" as the "last great boys' show," highlighting its blend of gross-out stunts and dangerous pranks that entertained a generation. It reflects on the franchise's history and its unique appeal, especially through figures like Steve-O.

items.title: The Souls of Animals
items.summary: Drawing from Gary Kowalski's "The Souls of Animals," this article explores the concept of animal spirituality, defining soul as a capacity for moral sense, appreciation of beauty, creativity, and self-awareness. It argues that by examining the inner lives of other creatures, humans deepen their own understanding of existence beyond mere survival.

items.title: Meta’s glasses will turn off the camera if you tamper with the privacy light
items.summary: Meta is updating its smart glasses to disable the camera if tampering with or destruction of the privacy LED light is detected, responding to public backlash and modder workarounds. This measure aims to address privacy concerns by preventing users from circumventing the visible recording indicator.

items.title: Netflix is about to host videos from BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, and other publishers
items.summary: Netflix will begin hosting video content from various digital media brands, including BuzzFeed and Condé Nast, starting August 3rd. This initiative aims to allow subscribers to watch licensed past videos and new ongoing series from across the internet without leaving the Netflix platform.

items.title: Of course viewers are giving up on Netflix shows
items.summary: Netflix is reportedly struggling with significant viewership drop-offs for its series after their first seasons, with shows like "Beef" losing 70% of its audience. The company is investigating reasons for this trend, which affects popular adaptations like "Avatar: The Last Airbender" and "One Piece."

items.title: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
items.summary: Samsung announced its next Galaxy Unpacked event for July 22nd, teasing a new, wider foldable phone format to expand its lineup. Alongside this new design, updated versions of the existing Flip and Fold phones are also expected.

items.title: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
items.summary: New Jersey lawmakers are considering a bill that could mandate specific sensor requirements, like lidar and radar, for autonomous vehicles, potentially banning Tesla's camera-only approach to robotaxis. This legislative effort seeks to settle the debate over the necessary sensor technology for driverless cars.

items.title: The Steam Machine fits my TV, my desk, and my life
items.summary: Despite its drawbacks, the reviewer would purchase Valve's Steam Machine for its ability to play hundreds of Steam library games and seamless cloud saves with the Steam Deck. It offers a unique gaming experience not replicated by other consoles like the PS5 or Xbox Series X.

items.title: The whole Pixel line could get more expensive this year
items.summary: Google's upcoming Pixel lineup, including the Pixel 11 phones and Pixel Watch 5, is rumored to have increased starting prices compared to previous generations. Reports suggest a $50 jump for the Pixel Watch 5 and potential removal of the 128GB storage option for Pixel 11 phones.

items.title: This jumping $800 robot camera dog filled me with joy
items.summary: The Mondo Robotics Beni is an agile, two-legged robot camera dog that can run, jump, and perform tricks, offering a unique alternative to drones. Despite needing further development, early demos of Beni suggest a fun and resilient robotic companion.
